title: Identity Fusion
why: There's a name for the thing where someone takes an argument about policy as an attack on their person. It's a measurable construct with 15 years of field data behind it, and the finding that matters runs opposite to intuition.
hook: Why does making someone feel personally threatened make them more loyal to the group, not less?

inspiration.what: An IG reel on political identity that sent me looking for the actual research

flagNote: Words that import the verdict into the premise. Cheaper to name now than to untangle later.
